glossary.name: Bulb
glossary.definition: A light bulb used to illuminate a light fixture. Available in a variety of shapes and wattages. PAR16 bulbs are included in some of our products.

glossary.name: Acrylic Cone
glossary.definition: An acrylic decorative cone designed to cover 1/8IPS pipes.

glossary.name: Arm
glossary.definition: A pipe that serves as an arm with a threaded exterior end that screws into hardware and contains an internal thread used for attachment to a surface such as a cabinet (first named “RA” for “reducer arm” in a smaller style).

glossary.name: Square Pipe
glossary.definition: A 1/4IPS square pipe used on geometric chandeliers. We ofte use “Q” to signify something is “quarter IPS” or “1/4 IPS”. The S refers to its square profile.

glossary.name: Wire Nut
glossary.definition: A twist-on connector used to join electrical wires securely and insulate the connection. Various colors hold different sizes and quantities of wires. Used inside bodies often and included in kits.

glossary.name: Wire, Ground
glossary.definition: Copper or tinned copper wire used to ground a fixture

glossary.name: Wire
glossary.definition: Conductive material used to supply power to the fixture, typically insulated for safety. Most often 18 gauge copper or tinned copper is used in lighting applications.

glossary.name: Wire Ferrule
glossary.definition: A small metal sleeve that crimps over the end of a wire to ensure a secure connection.

glossary.name: Wire Connector
glossary.definition: A device used to securely join multiple electrical wires, ensuring a safe and insulated connection via a crimp-on fitting attached to the end of a wire called a wire ferrule.

glossary.name: Washer
glossary.definition: A flat disk used to distribute the load of a screw or nut, preventing damage to surfaces or components.

glossary.name: Thread Adapter
glossary.definition: A component with two sizes of male thread connected together used to add threads to a hole or connect different sizes of female threaded parts.

glossary.name: Swivel
glossary.definition: A threaded connector with two parts adjoined by an internal spring or set screws that installs between parts of a fixture to allow it to be repositioned.

glossary.name: Switch
glossary.definition: A mechanical or electronic device that controls the electrical current, dimming the light or turning it on/off.

glossary.name: Spine, Urchin
glossary.definition: A spine of various lengths screwed into the body of an urchin-style fixture. Hundreds of these spines combine to create the characteristic urchin fixture shape.

glossary.name: Strain Relief
glossary.definition: A device used to prevent tension or pulling forces from damaging wires at their connections. Prevents supply wires and plug-in cords from being pulled out easily.

glossary.name: Set Screw
glossary.definition: A short, headless screw used to secure components in place without protruding, often used to prevent turning. Used with hex key.

glossary.name: Slip Ring
glossary.definition: A ring used with a set screw to secure parts along the length of a rod or against another part.

glossary.name: Sputnik Core
glossary.definition: The central hub of a multi-arm "sputnik-style" light fixture. Consists of two halves creating a sphere.

glossary.name: Socket
glossary.definition: The electrical component that holds the light bulb and provides the necessary connection for power. Contains tab, shell, insulation, and thread, flange, or wings for attachment.

glossary.name: Sleeve
glossary.definition: A protective tube or covering for wires ensuring insulation and durability.

glossary.name: Shade
glossary.definition: A cover that surrounds a bulb to diffuse, soften, or direct light. Often made of acrylic, glass, metal, or fabric.

glossary.name: Screw
glossary.definition: A threaded fastener used to join or secure parts of a fixture. Can be flathead, Phillips, hexagonal, among others. Included often in kits.

glossary.name: Rod Knob
glossary.definition: A 1-inch long cylindrical rod / knob with one threaded hole drilled in the middle toward the center used to hold an arm.

glossary.name: Rod
glossary.definition: A solid cylindrical rod of varying lengths with threaded holes near ends drilled toward the center to hold arms for drawer pulls.

glossary.name: Ring
glossary.definition: A decorative, structural circular manifold used to affix several pipes together parallel to form a larger fixture.

glossary.name: Reducer with Shoulder
glossary.definition: A connector with shoulder with external male thread and a full length of female thread running inside. Used to reduce or increase the size of a thread with a shoulder to stop part when screwing in.

glossary.name: Reducer
glossary.definition: A connector with a full length of male thread outside and a full length of female thread running inside. Used to reduce or increase the size of a thread.

glossary.name: Ring Body
glossary.definition: A circular structural element with slip holes directed perpendicularly and used to arrange pipes holding sockets. Open to top and bottom and enclosed using check rings or caps.

glossary.name: Reducer Arm
glossary.definition: A pipe that serves as an arm with a threaded exterior end that screws into hardware and contains an internal thread used for attachment to a surface such as a cabinet (later renamed “AR” in a larger style).

glossary.name: Pipe U-Shaped Female
glossary.definition: A U-shaped pipe with female threads on both ends machined with set screw holes near the ends.

glossary.name: Pipe Tee
glossary.definition: A connector pipe used to create a 3-arm configurations in a T-shape.

glossary.name: Pipe Male Bent
glossary.definition: A bent pipe with external threads, used for connecting to female-threaded components. Used most often in hardware.

glossary.name: Pipe Male
glossary.definition: A pipe with external threads, used for connecting to female-threaded components. Often connected with couplings.

glossary.name: Plug
glossary.definition: An electrical part which connects to the end of a fixture’s hot, neutral, and/or ground wires and is used to insert into a wall outlet to energize the fixture.

glossary.name: Pipe Square, 1/4IPS
glossary.definition: A square pipe with 1/4IPS female threaded ends and set screw holes. The "Q" indicates 1/4IPS, and the parts are used with the BOS square bodies.

glossary.name: Pipe Female Bent
glossary.definition: A bent pipe with internal threads near each end used for connecting to male-threaded components in fixture assembly. Often used as a hook or to redirect a pipe or light fixture arm.

glossary.name: Pipe Female
glossary.definition: A pipe with internal threads near each end used for connecting to male-threaded components in fixture assembly. Common connected seamlessly with threaded nipples to form longer sections.

glossary.name: Pipe Custom
glossary.definition: A pipe designed specifically for unique configurations in custom products where the pipe may have various arrangements of threaded holes.

glossary.name: Pipe Aluminum Thick
glossary.definition: A pipe designed as a body with threaded ends and threaded holes along its side to attach arms and canopy. Includes FPS2 finial plug on each end.

glossary.name: Pipe Aluminum
glossary.definition: An aluminum pipe with female threaded ends often with set screw holes on the side ends and larger threaded holes to attach other pipes via neck breaks.

glossary.name: Nozzle
glossary.definition: A female threaded cylinder attached at the base with a male thread. Used to add longer lengths or depths of thread.

glossary.name: Nipple
glossary.definition: A short threaded pipe (often threaded the entire length) used to connect sockets, canopies, or other fixture and hardware parts.

glossary.name: Neck Break
glossary.definition: A circular connector with a center slip hole where wire runs through. The part forms a seamless connection between the flat end of one pipe and the round side of another pipe eliminating gaps.

glossary.name: Nut
glossary.definition: A fastening component with internal threading, used to secure nipples, reducers, rods, and other threaded parts in a fixture assembly. Used functionally and/or as decorative accents.

glossary.name: Mounting Plate
glossary.definition: A plate used as a base for attaching fixtures securely to electrical boxes or surfaces. Often configured to be “universal” by providing several slots or holes to work with a variety of electrical boxes.

glossary.name: Mount
glossary.definition: A bracket or structure with a central hole/wire way designed for containing wire while cleanly and securely attaching rounded fixtures to flat shapes such as canopies.

glossary.name: Lock Washer
glossary.definition: A washer designed to prevent threaded parts such as nipples, screws, or nuts from loosening over time due to vibration or movement.

glossary.name: Lug
glossary.definition: A circular or spade-shaped metal terminal used to crimp onto electrical wire (usually bare copper or green insulation wire) to provide a grounding point.

glossary.name: Lamp Cup
glossary.definition: A decorative cylindrical or conical cover that slides over a socket and attaches on a fixture on one side and is open on the other side where a bulb or lamp is inserted.

glossary.name: Body Lid
glossary.definition: A body cover that screws onto a body enclosure or attaches via screws. Typically removable to access internal wiring.

glossary.name: Label
glossary.definition: A branded or instructional sticker applied to fixtures, providing product details, safety warnings, or certification marks.

glossary.name: Knob
glossary.definition: A decorative or functional grip used to adjust or turn on/off a switch.

glossary.name: Kit
glossary.definition: A pre-assembled set of components packaged together for specific product lines, ensuring easy assembly and installation.

glossary.name: Hang Tag String
glossary.definition: String used to attach hang tags. Often black, gold, or orange.

glossary.name: Hang Tag
glossary.definition: A tag attached to the fixture during production or packaging that includes branding, product information, or installation instructions.

glossary.name: Hang-Straight
glossary.definition: A swiveling device that allows ceiling-mounted fixtures to hang vertically when mounted on angled or sloped ceilings.

glossary.name: Holder
glossary.definition: A plate or ring used inside a globe opening to serve as a seat to hold a globe in place. Typically pairs with a globe cap and slip ring/coupling.

glossary.name: Hex Key
glossary.definition: An L-shaped tool used for affixing set screws with hex shaped holes.

glossary.name: Hickey
glossary.definition: A threaded fitting used to join fixture parts (like pipes) creating an opening for accessing wiring from the pipes.

glossary.name: Globe Cap
glossary.definition: A cap that secures a globe to a fixture via a holder, ensuring stability and a finished look. May also be used as a decorative element or canopy.

glossary.name: Gas Attachment
glossary.definition: Attachment with bendable arm for affixing a socket to a hole in the side wall of a shade while also containing a wire way hole for running wire from the socket to the rest of the fixture.

glossary.name: Globe
glossary.definition: A glass spherical or rounded shade/cover that encloses and diffuses light from a light bulb.

glossary.name: Finial Plug with Shoulder
glossary.definition: A threaded closure with shoulder used to seal threaded open holes in a fixture or serve as a decorative accent. The shoulder serves as a stopping point when screwing in the finial plug.

glossary.name: Finial Plug
glossary.definition: A threaded closure used to seal threaded open holes in a fixture or serve as a decorative accent.

glossary.name: Finial Cap
glossary.definition: A decorative end cap or closure for securing lampshades or other elements of a light fixture or hardware.

glossary.name: Dome
glossary.definition: A rounded or hemispherical shade used to soften or block light. Typically contains a socket inside and is used to direct light in the direction of its opening.

glossary.name: Disc
glossary.definition: A decorative element with a flat, disc shape, most often acrylic.

glossary.name: Connector, Y
glossary.definition: A Y-shaped fitting used to join three pipes, rods, or wires, enabling multi-directional connections in custom fixture designs.

glossary.name: Check Ring
glossary.definition: A circular component used to secure parts together or cover an opening, ensuring items remain in place while also serving to form a clean, finished look.

glossary.name: Collar
glossary.definition: A circular component that secures shades, globes, or other parts to the fixture. Often threaded to screw onto pipes or sockets.

glossary.name: Crossbar
glossary.definition: A flat metal bar that attaches to an electrical box and serves as a mounting platform for fixtures. Includes threaded holes for securing the fixture to the box.

glossary.name: Canopy
glossary.definition: A decorative cover used to conceal the electrical box and wiring at the mounting point of a ceiling or wall fixture. Can be round, square, or custom-shaped.

glossary.name: Coupling
glossary.definition: A connector used to join two threaded pipes or rods, enabling customization of fixture size and socket placement. Also used for attaching fixtures to surfaces on hardware.

glossary.name: Box
glossary.definition: For our purposes, “BX” is a part number for a paper or cardboard (often branded) box used for packaging products.

glossary.name: Urchin Body
glossary.definition: A complex CNC machined body and lid containing threaded holes for securing sockets and urchin spines for urchin fixture.

glossary.name: Bushing
glossary.definition: A protective ring or sleeve used to insulate and protect wires where they pass through metal openings, preventing wear or damage to the insulation.

glossary.name: Bullet Shade
glossary.definition: A narrow, conical-shaped shade that focuses light in a specific direction, commonly used in task or spot lighting.

glossary.name: Bracket
glossary.definition: A structural support used to mount fixtures to walls or ceilings. Often designed with multiple holes to adjust the mounting position.

glossary.name: Body
glossary.definition: The main structural part of a light fixture that houses wires and wire connectors to connect sockets to the supply wires which run to the junction box. Serves as a foundation for attaching other parts. Typically consists of a body enclosure and lid.

glossary.name: Bead
glossary.definition: A decorative accent screwed onto the end of a rod used on an urchin light fixture.

glossary.name: Anchor
glossary.definition: A device used to secure fixtures to walls, ceilings, or other surfaces. Typically used in conjunction with screws to provide strong support for overhead or wall-mounted installations.
